title: abutilon aurantiacum
intro: Abutilon aurantiacum, also known as orange mallow or orange cottonweed, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and cotton-like seeds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Abutilon aurantiacum has been found to have various health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of digestive health, and potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as ointments and salves, to treat wounds and skin conditions.
